changes: Added applicant-name line normalization to `src/hanah_tax_ocr/ocr/engine.py` so region OCR can rescue a strong single-line name from noisy multi-line applicant crops without changing unrelated document types.
Added `tests/test_ocr_engine.py` coverage for the new applicant-name line-selection fallback and kept the probe seed/materialization tests from the previous iteration green.

Validated the candidate against the bottom `edge_overlap` withholding probe, the existing 3-case real regression slice, and a hybrid official eval that reuses the exact `current_official_v17` synthetic inputs for non-withholding cases while rerunning only the affected withholding cases.

invalid_rematerialized_full_eval_observa.note: A naive full rerun with newly materialized synthetic residency inputs showed `issue_date` regressions, but the same-input hybrid replay removed them completely; the hybrid replay is the authoritative comparison for this iteration.

kept: document-level `withholding_edge_overlap_bottom_probe` as a tracked applicant/middle-name regression probe
edge-overlap augmentation delta record showing english-name recovery without date regression
discarded: the earlier noisy applicant-name scoring candidate from iteration 07 that produced `To S. CHEN`
the rematerialized full-eval regression signal for residency synthetic dates as a decision input, because it used different generated inputs than the baseline `v17` run

next_priorities: Extend the same line-selection rescue idea to a dedicated `middle_name` strategy or crop expansion, since the top-table `middle_name` region is still effectively blank on the real withholding template.

Add one more semi-real withholding probe that perturbs the upper name row directly, so applicant-name rescue and middle-name rescue can be separated instead of being entangled in the bottom signature block.
